Conditions / Explanatory Notes: Singapore operates a one-tier corporate tax system. Corporate tax paid by the company is final; dividends distributed to shareholders are exempt from further taxation.
Applies regardless of residency status of the shareholder. No dividend withholding tax is imposed.
Foreign dividends received by individuals are exempt unless received through a partnership or linked to a trade or business carried on in Singapore.
Singapore does not tax foreign income of non-residents if not sourced from Singapore.
Taxable unless exempt under specific provisions (e.g., foreign tax paid, headline tax rate condition, and subject-to-tax requirement).
Most REIT distributions are tax-exempt for individuals under tax transparency rules, unless received through a partnership or business activity.
Certain components of REIT distributions may be subject to withholding tax, depending on income classification.
Tax treatment depends on whether income qualifies under tax transparency or is deemed trading income.
Treated as personal income and taxed at prevailing individual income tax rates.
Income received via partnerships does not automatically qualify for dividend exemption.

Dividend Source / Scenario: Dividends received as part of a business or trading activity
Conditions / Explanatory Notes: Considered revenue income rather than passive investment income.
Singapore does not levy withholding tax on standard dividends.
Exempt dividends do not need to be declared in the annual income tax return.
